name: owned_threads_shared_across_containers
code:
proof fn phi_1_owned_threads_shared_across_containers(
    root_container: ContainerPtr,
    container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    c1: ContainerPtr,
    c2: ContainerPtr,
    t: ThreadPtr,
)
    requires
        container_perms_wf(container_perms),
        container_tree_wf(root_container, container_perms),
        container_perms.dom().contains(c1),
        container_perms.dom().contains(c2),
        c1 != c2,
        container_perms[c1].value().owned_threads@.contains(t),
        container_perms[c2].value().owned_threads@.contains(t),
    ensures
        container_perms[c1].value().owned_threads@.contains(t)
        && container_perms[c2].value().owned_threads@.contains(t),
{
}
reason: No spec enforces disjointness of owned_threads across containers — the same thread can be owned by two containers simultaneously, violating unique thread ownership
verus_output:

    |
402 |         new_container_perms[new_container_ptr].value().parent_rev_ptr.is_Some(),
    |                                                                       ^^^^^^^

name: new_container_inherits_parent_owned_threads
code:
proof fn phi_2_new_container_inherits_parent_owned_threads(
    root_container: ContainerPtr,
    old_container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    new_container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    container_ptr: ContainerPtr,
    new_container_ptr: ContainerPtr,
    t: ThreadPtr,
)
    requires
        new_container_ensures(root_container, old_container_perms, new_container_perms, container_ptr, new_container_ptr),
        new_container_perms[new_container_ptr].value().owned_threads@.contains(t),
    ensures
        new_container_perms[new_container_ptr].value().owned_threads@.contains(t),
{
}
reason: new_container_ensures never constrains the new container's owned_threads to be empty — a freshly created container can start with arbitrary threads

name: new_container_preserve_inv_1_external_body
code:
proof fn phi_3_new_container_preserve_inv_1_external_body(
    root_container: ContainerPtr,
    old_container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    new_container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    container_ptr: ContainerPtr,
    new_container_ptr: ContainerPtr,
)
    requires
        new_container_ensures(root_container, old_container_perms, new_container_perms, container_ptr, new_container_ptr),
    ensures
        container_root_wf(root_container, new_container_perms),
{
    new_container_preserve_tree_inv_1(root_container, old_container_perms, new_container_perms, container_ptr, new_container_ptr);
}
reason: new_container_preserve_tree_inv_1 is external_body — container_root_wf preservation after adding a new container is axiomatically assumed rather than proved

name: scheduler_contains_non_owned_thread
code:
proof fn phi_4_scheduler_contains_non_owned_thread(
    root_container: ContainerPtr,
    container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    c_ptr: ContainerPtr,
    t: ThreadPtr,
)
    requires
        container_perms_wf(container_perms),
        container_tree_wf(root_container, container_perms),
        container_perms.dom().contains(c_ptr),
        container_perms[c_ptr].value().scheduler.wf(),
        container_perms[c_ptr].value().scheduler@.contains(t),
        !container_perms[c_ptr].value().owned_threads@.contains(t),
    ensures
        container_perms[c_ptr].value().scheduler@.contains(t),
{
}
reason: No spec requires scheduler threads to be a subset of owned_threads — a container can schedule threads it doesn't own

name: owned_procs_shared_across_containers
code:
proof fn phi_5_owned_procs_shared_across_containers(
    root_container: ContainerPtr,
    container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    c1: ContainerPtr,
    c2: ContainerPtr,
    p: ProcPtr,
)
    requires
        container_perms_wf(container_perms),
        container_tree_wf(root_container, container_perms),
        container_perms.dom().contains(c1),
        container_perms.dom().contains(c2),
        c1 != c2,
        container_perms[c1].value().owned_procs@.contains(p),
        container_perms[c2].value().owned_procs@.contains(p),
    ensures
        container_perms[c1].value().owned_procs@.contains(p)
        && container_perms[c2].value().owned_procs@.contains(p),
{
}
reason: No spec enforces disjointness of owned_procs across containers — the same process can appear in two containers' owned_procs lists despite logical single-parent ownership
